help.title: Help & Support
help.subtitle: Find answers, read documentation, or contact our support team.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.description: Comprehensive guides, API references, and integration tutorials.

email.title: Email Support
email.description: Detailed help for complex issues. Usually replied within one business day.

chat.title: Live Chat
chat.description: Real-time support from our technical team — typically responds within minutes.

items.question: What programming languages are supported?
items.answer: Clone Lens supports Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, C/C++, C#, Go, Ruby, PHP, Swift, and Kotlin. Semantic and AI-based analysis can detect patterns across different languages.

items.question: How does clone detection work?
items.answer: The system uses four layers: token-based analysis for Type 1 & 2 clones, text similarity for near-duplicates, AST graph comparison for structural patterns, and AI-driven semantic analysis for functionally equivalent but syntactically different code.

items.question: What file types can I upload?
items.answer: Individual code files (.java, .js, .ts, .py, etc.), ZIP archives of full projects, or Excel/CSV files containing code snippets with configurable row selection.

items.question: How is the similarity score calculated?
items.answer: The overall score is a weighted average of token, text, AST graph, and AI similarity scores. Weights are determined dynamically based on code characteristics to maximize accuracy.

items.question: Can I integrate this with my CI/CD pipeline?
items.answer: Yes. Clone Lens exposes API endpoints you can call from any CI/CD system. Set similarity thresholds to fail builds when code duplication exceeds acceptable levels.

items.question: Is my code secure when I upload it?
items.answer: All code is encrypted in transit and at rest. We never share your code with third parties. Enterprise customers can request on-premises deployment for complete data sovereignty.
